blockedAssertions.blockedBy: The repo proves successful rebase+push and the post-rebase push-failure classification/message branches separately, but still lacks an integrated harness that forces a real push transport failure after a successful `git pull --rebase` and verifies the preserved rebased state end-to-end.

appliedUpdates.description: Added validation concurrency limits and flow-validator guidance for web-ui and tauri-rust-cli surfaces.
Recorded rerun results after the frontend became reachable: resolved the previously blocked AI assertions and kept VAL-PUSH-012 blocked with refined evidence.

frictions: The shared root UI at http://localhost:4158 serves the onboarding/browser shell, so AI settings validation still relied heavily on source-backed analysis even after reachability was restored.

The tauri-rust-cli validation surface is guidance-limited to source inspection plus existing automated verification; no real Tauri runtime harness is available here for observing live toasts or inducing post-rebase transport failures.
`cargo test` must be run from `apps/desktop/src-tauri` via `--manifest-path`; running it from the repo root fails because there is no top-level Cargo.toml.
blockers: VAL-PUSH-012 requires a controllable push failure after a successful rebase in a real Tauri/runtime-backed flow; existing tests do not cover that scenario and browser mode cannot exercise git push.
